outcome_summary: Twelfth routine fire of the T-SEED-QA seed-graph QA census. Scored shard 12 â€” 27 pedagogical_prerequisite edges (C1) + 20 nodes (C2/C3) per the pinned seed_qa_audit.md rubric â€” and wrote seed_qa_evidence/shard_12.md. Findings: C1 0/27 defective = 0.0% (the census's SEVENTH 0-defect shard after 04/06/07/09/10/11; three in a row across 10/11/12); 24 Sound, 3 Defensible (E-5 tropesâ†’bundle_theory trope-variant only â€” Williams 1953 trope-bundle theory takes tropes as ontological building blocks while Hume's bundle theory of mind used impressions/ideas and Russell's used universals; E-11 formal_proofâ†’classical_logic structural-vs-pedagogical shape â€” eighth-consecutive-shard instance of 'target is the more pedagogically-familiar concrete concept,' held at Defensible on the structural-priority reading; E-13 DNâ†’IBE NEW SHAPE for the routine batch â€” two parallel theories of scientific explanation with historical-and-dialectical succession but weak conceptual prereq), 0 Reversed, 0 Weak-redundant. C2: 0/20 fail. C3: 0/20 fail â€” second consecutive quadruple-0 shard. Zero audit-touched edges (programmatic exact-and-reverse tuple cross-reference against assertion-block tuple lists in 0061â€“0065 returned zero matches; the lowest audit footprint in the routine batch â€” vs shard 11's 5/27 = 18.5% and shard 10's 2/27 = 7.4% â€” making shard 12 the cleanest fresh-authoring drift check available). Cumulative C1 across 01â€“12: 23/326 = 7.1% (down from 7.7%), continuing to tick DOWN under the 13% baseline as the sample grows. NOVEL pattern for the routine batch: duplicate-prereq shape â€” E-12 deontologyâ†’supererogation AND E-26 normative_ethicsâ†’supererogation reach the same target via distinct framework-prereqs (specific-deontological-theory vs broader-normative-ethics-umbrella; Urmson 1958 originally framed supererogation as a problem for any normative theory with a notion of duty, not just deontology); neither strictly subsumes the other; flag for SQA-20 to determine whether the graph systematically carries both specific-and-broader prereq pathways for ethical-concept targets. Four borderline-PASS C3 calls (N-3 formal_proof 'inference rule' unglossed but structural shape parsable; N-10 sensitivity_condition 'possible world' unglossed but counterfactual rephrase rescues; N-15 character_and_content heavy technical vocab in opening but 'I'-example rescue inline; N-16 externalism_epistemic 'justification-conferring factors' jargon BUT 'reliability of the belief-forming process' gloss is in the SAME sentence). All four held at PASS on established calibration. The Defensible cluster sub-shapes are now distinguishable: 'discipline â†’ object-of-study' (shards 05â€“10), 'umbrella â†’ specific instance' (across multiple shards), and now 'parallel theories with historical succession' (E-13 this shard). Three distinct Defensible flavors all held at Defensible per established calibration. Five non-trivial judgment calls in the session (recorded in detail in the evidence file): E-5 tropesâ†’bundle_theory variant scope, E-11 formal_proofâ†’classical_logic structural vs pedagogical, E-13 DNâ†’IBE new parallel-theories shape, E-18 deontologyâ†’kantian_ethics categorial-umbrella-to-instance per shard 10 calibration, E-26 normative_ethicsâ†’supererogation duplicate-prereq Sound-not-Weak-redundant. Candidate findings only; disposition is the SQA-20 closeout. SQA-12 marked complete (status flip staged with the close commit per Issue #122 lifecycle-push-wrapper sequencing); SQA-13 next. No in-band Issues. 0 validator hard-fails (50 checks on --final-check). mempalace_zero_citations_after_search fires for the same benign-shape reason as prior census shards: the boot search returned 0 drawers above the 0.60 threshold (no prior-context drawers exist for this specific routine-task pattern), and the diary entry references prior shards (10/11) by named-session reference rather than drawer-ID citation. mempalace_diary_read_late did NOT fire this session â€” diary read was via the MCP tool surface at boot before plan authoring (the canonical path), unlike S-0173's python-direct read path.
